hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: You raised before the cards were dealt. Your opponent called.
Flop — Ace, 2, Ace. You bet. Your opponent raised you back.
River — Ace. Your opponent shoved all-in without hesitation.

wrongReasons: 5 4 is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than 5 4 would have.
Q 2 makes a full house here — a hand that strong would have raised earlier and pushed harder for maximum value. The measured sizing in this hand points to something weaker.
3 J is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than 3 J would have.
hand.explanation:
Answer: 6 3

Your opponent flopped three of a kind and raised the flop immediately to build the pot. Sets are too strong to slow-play — they pushed their advantage on every street.
